cataloging_subject.label: Political, Religious, and Love Poems (U-M encoded selection, pp. xlv-292)
cataloging_subject.scope_note: The cataloging subject is the 1903 edition-defined selection represented by the U-M encoding: title page, correction note, and pp. xlv-292. Its 118 direct DIV2 containers are counted once as edition representation units, not normalized abstract medieval works. Omitted front matter, pp. 293-342, and back matter are outside the subject.

catalog_summary.title: Political, Religious, and Love Poems (U-M encoded selection, pp. xlv-292)
catalog_summary.author: Anonymous and named Middle English writers; item-level attributions include John Lydgate, Alain Chartier / Sir Richard Ros, Henry Baradoun, and William Huchen
catalog_summary.date: Component texts c. 1200-1531; edition-defined selection revised 1903
catalog_summary.region: Unassigned; component composition contexts are variable or uncertain

titles.value: Political, Religious, and Love Poems (U-M encoded selection, pp. xlv-292)

scope.description: Edition-defined U-M selection
Main title printed on the 1903 title page; the full title page advertises additional material omitted from this cataloging subject

agents.name: Anonymous and unidentified contributors
notes: This is a cataloging collective, not a claim of common authorship.

scope.description: 1903 edition-defined selection
Selection and arrangement of the edited materials
Lydgate's Horse, Goose, and Sheep
First version headed 'By Lydgate'
French source of La Belle Dame sans Merci
English translation of La Belle Dame sans Merci
The Prentise unto Woe
Only the signed Hymn to the Virgin text

notes: No broader authorship of New College MS 320 is asserted.

scope.description: Transcript/proof contribution for item 5.75
Anonymous or unidentified material across the remaining containers and within partially attributed containers

notes: This collective does not imply common authorship.

scope.description: Broad range for heterogeneous component texts, not a single work date
First publication of Furnivall's edition lineage
Revision defining the selected edition representation cataloged here
Primary language of the 118 numbered intellectual item containers; title-page, correction-note, and structural records are excluded

language_statements.label: Latin

scope.description: Substantive Latin refrains, formulae, definitions, and prose framing intrinsic to these edition containers; Latin confined to headings, editorial rubrics, or incidental short quotations is excluded

notes: Modern English editorial matter is not promoted to a viewer language facet.

scope.description: Selection contains prose-only, verse-only, and mixed containers
Seven count-bearing prose-only edition containers
Ninety-four count-bearing verse-only edition containers
Seventeen count-bearing mixed prose-and-verse edition containers
Political verse in the five identified edition containers
Devotional verse and mixed verse-centered devotional material in the identified edition containers
Love poetry and courtly verse in the identified edition containers
Political, social, anticlerical, or moral satire in the identified edition containers
Instructional, admonitory, proverbial, and practical didactic material in the identified edition containers
Medical recipes, remedies, and explanatory medical writing in the four identified edition containers
The identified rat-expulsion verse charm, Rats Away
Christian devotion, Passion, penitence, salvation, and related moral theology in the identified edition containers
Death, mortality, sin, judgment, and preparation for death in the identified edition containers

subject_statements.label: Politics, kingship, governance, and social satire

scope.description: Politics, kingship, governance, estates, and social critique in the identified edition containers
Love and courtly discourse in the identified edition containers
Household, medical, charm, calendrical, and practical knowledge in the identified edition containers

summaries.value: A heterogeneous Furnivall-edited selection of political, devotional, love, satirical, didactic, practical, and medical texts, mostly Middle English with embedded Latin, transmitted by numerous manuscript and print witnesses.

scope.description: Cataloging subject

summaries.value: The encoding retains a title page and correction note, followed by 42 containers under Political, Religious, and Love Poems and 76 under Short Religious Poems. Sixteen nested version/part records support navigation but do not add to the 118-item count.

scope.description: Encoded selection structure

summaries.value: The title page advertises additional romance material and the full 1903 volume contains front/back matter and pp. 293-342 that are not encoded. Those omitted materials, including Rossetti's notes and John Metham material, are boundary evidence rather than content parts.

scope.description: Edition/encoding boundary

extent.description: Counts are over the 118 direct DIV2 edition representation containers. The 773 LG and 7,200 non-note L totals are XML encoding-structure measurements, not normalized abstract-work metrics. Four structural rows count zero; sixteen overlapping DIV3 children count null.

related_works.title: Political, Religious, and Love Poems (1866 first edition)
notes: The cataloging subject is the 1903 revised edition-defined selection.

related_works.title: Peare of Provence and the Fair Maguelone (fragment)
notes: Advertised on the title page and present in omitted pp. 293-342; not part of the U-M selection.

related_works.title: The Knight Amoryus and the Lady Cleopes (John Metham sketch, prologue, and epilogue)
notes: Advertised on the title page and present in omitted pp. 293-342; not part of the U-M selection.

evidence.citation: Element-count and content-part inspection of ant9912.xml
evidence.locator: TITLEPAGE; DIV1 2, 4, 5; 118 DIV2 and 16 DIV3 nodes
evidence.summary: Supports the edition-selection boundary, forms, and exact 118/138 count model.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Furnivall 1903 and its 1866 predecessor
evidence.summary: Supports edition identity, revision date, and omitted-volume boundary.

evidence.citation: Linked lineage evidence for Furnivall 1866 first edition
evidence.summary: Direct 1866 Internet Archive facsimile evidence supporting the first-edition publication date.
Supports named manuscript and printed sources and their item-level scopes.

open_questions.question: Should the 118 edition containers later be normalized into abstract medieval works?
next_steps: Keep the current edition-container count stable; undertake separate work-identity research before adding any normalized-work layer.

open_questions.question: Can anonymous components receive more granular dates, places, genres, and attributions?
next_steps: Research components individually against later scholarship while retaining evidence-scoped assertions.

notes: William Michael Rossetti's notes belong to omitted front matter/full-volume context and are not a responsibility over the encoded selection.
John Metham material advertised on the title page is omitted and therefore not a content part.
William Huchen is scoped only to the signed hymn in item 5.76.
Modern English editorial apparatus is described but not exposed as a language facet.
The label [Nota de mirabilibus mundi] is supplied for otherwise headless item 5.9.

content_parts.label: Edition title page

content_parts.label: Correction note
content_parts.summary: Furnivall's short correction locating Falmouth in Cornwall rather than Dorsetshire.
